min_version.description: Minimum version required on the "Shoreline" backend (API server).

retries.description: Number of retries for API calls, in case of e.g. transient network failures.

token.description: Customer/user-specific authorization token for the "Shoreline" API server. May be provided via `SHORELINE_TOKEN` env variable.

url.description: Customer-specific URL for the "Shoreline" API server.

command.description: The command to execute for this action

complete_long_template.description: **Deprecated** Long template for completion notifications (deprecated - server controlled)

error_long_template.description: **Deprecated** Long template for error notifications (deprecated - server controlled)

name.description: The name of the action

shell.description: Shell to use for command execution

start_long_template.description: **Deprecated** Long template for start notifications (deprecated - server controlled)

condition_type.description: **Deprecated** Kind of check in an Alarm (e.g. above or below) vs a threshold for a Metric.

condition_value.description: **Deprecated** Switching value (threshold) for a Metric in an Alarm.

description.description: A user-friendly explanation of an object.

enabled.description: If the object is currently enabled or disabled.

family.description: General class for an Action or Bot (e.g., custom, standard, metric, or system check).

fire_long_template.description: **Deprecated** The long description of the Alarm's triggering condition.

fire_query.description: The trigger condition for an Alarm (general expression) or the TimeTrigger (e.g. 'every 5m').

fire_short_template.description: The short description of the Alarm's triggering condition.

fire_title_template.description: UI title of the Alarm's triggering condition.

name.description: The name/symbol for the object within backend and the op language (must be unique, only alphanumeric/underscore).

raise_for.description: **Deprecated** Where an Alarm is raised (e.g., local to a resource, or global to the system).

resolve_long_template.description: **Deprecated** The long description of the Alarm's resolution.

resolve_short_template.description: The short description of the Alarm's resolution.

resolve_title_template.description: UI title of the Alarm's' resolution.

resource_query.description: A set of Resources (e.g. host, pod, container), optionally filtered on tags or dynamic conditions.

resource_type.description: The type of object (i.e., Alarm, Action, Bot, Resource, or File).

block.description: A condition that triggers Alerts or Actions.

command.description: The bot command in the format 'if <alarm> then <action> fi'.

name.description: The name/symbol for the object within backend and the op language (must be unique, only alphanumeric/underscore).

trigger_id.description: The ID of the trigger. It's required when linking an **external** trigger to an execution entity. If it's not set, then the id of the internal trigger entity will be saved in the state file.

trigger_source.description: The source of the trigger. It's required when linking an **external** trigger to an execution entity (for example: `ALERTMANAGER`). If it's not set, then the source of the internal trigger entity will be saved in the state file.

block.description: An automation that ties an Action to an Alert.

groups.description: The JSON encoded groups of the dashboard.

groups_full.description: Complete groups configuration returned by the API, including server-added fields. Shows diffs when external drift is detected and when configuration changes.

name.description: The name of the group.

groups_list.description: The groups of the dashboard as a native Terraform list. Provides better plan changes and drift detection than the deprecated `groups` JSON string. Cannot be used together with `groups`.

name.description: The name of the dashboard

resource_query.description: The resource query for the dashboard

values.description: The JSON encoded values of the dashboard.

values_full.description: Complete values configuration returned by the API, including server-added fields. Shows diffs when external drift is detected and when configuration changes.

color.description: The color of the value group.

values_list.description: The values of the dashboard as a native Terraform list. Provides better plan changes and drift detection than the deprecated `values` JSON string. Cannot be used together with `values`.

block.description: Dashboard resource for creating dashboards with groups and values

checksum.description: Cryptographic hash (e.g. md5) of a File Resource. This is used to verify the integrity of the file when it is uploaded.

description.description: A user-friendly explanation of an object.

destination_path.description: Target location for a copied distributed File object.

enabled.description: If the object is currently enabled or disabled.

file_data.description: The destination URL where the file was uploaded to.

file_length.description: Length, in bytes, of a distributed File object.

inline_data.description: The inline file data of a distributed File object. (conflicts with input_file)

input_file.description: The local source of a distributed File object. (conflicts with inline_data)

md5.description: The md5 checksum of a file, e.g. filemd5("${path.module}/data/example-file.txt"). It's used to trigger the file upload when the file changes.

mode.description: The File's permissions, like 'chmod', in octal (e.g. '0644').

name.description: The name/symbol for the object within the platform and the op language (must be unique, only alphanumeric/underscore).

resource_query.description: A set of Resources (e.g. host, pod, container), optionally filtered on tags or dynamic conditions.

block.description: A datafile that is automatically copied/distributed to defined Resources.

api_certificate.description: The API certificate of the integration

api_key.description: The API key of the integration

api_rate_limit.description: The API rate limit of the integration

api_url.description: The API URL of the integration

cache_ttl_ms.description: The cache TTL of the integration in milliseconds

client_id.description: The client ID of the integration

client_secret.description: The client secret of the integration

external_url.description: The external URL of the integration

idp_name.description: The IDP name of the integration

jwt_auth_path.description: The JWT auth path of the integration

name.description: The name of the integration

payload_paths.description: The payload paths of the integration

permissions_user.description: The permissions user of the integration

role_name.description: The role name of the integration

serial_number.description: The serial number of the integration

tenant_id.description: The tenant ID of the integration

block.description: Integration resource for configuring integrations with external services

allowed_entities.description: The list of users who can run a runbook. Any user can run if left empty.

allowed_resources_query.description: The list of resources on which a runbook can run. No restriction, if left empty.

approvers.description: List of users who can approve runbook execution

category.description: Specifies the category for this runbook. To use categories, make sure your platform administrator has enabled the `ENABLE_RUNBOOK_CATEGORIES` setting. Once enabled, you can organize your runbooks by assigning them a category.

cells.description: The data cells inside a runbook. Defined as a list of JSON objects encoded in base64. These may be either Markdown or Op commands. Shows diffs only when configuration changes.

cells_full.description: Complete cells configuration returned by the API, including server-added fields. Shows diffs when external drift is detected and when configuration changes.

md.description: The Markdown content for this cell. Mutually exclusive with `op`.

name.description: The name of the cell.

op.description: The Op command content for this cell. Mutually exclusive with `md`.

cells_list.description: The data cells inside a runbook as a native Terraform list. Each cell is either a Markdown cell (set `md`) or an Op command cell (set `op`). Only one of `op` or `md` must be set per cell. Provides better plan changes and drift detection than the deprecated `cells` JSON string. Cannot be used together with `cells`.

communication_approval_notifications.description: Enables slack notifications for approval operations

communication_channel.description: A string value denoting the slack channel where notifications related to the runbook should be sent to

communication_cud_notifications.description: Enables slack notifications for create/update/delete operations

communication_execution_notifications.description: Enables slack notifications for runbook executions

communication_workspace.description: A string value denoting the slack workspace where notifications related to the runbook should be sent to

data.description: JSON-encoded string containing the runbook data. This can be loaded from a file using the `file()` function, e.g., `data = file("${path.module}/runbook.json")`. Unlike other JSON fields (params, cells, external_params), this field only stores what the user sets and does not have a corresponding _full attribute.

editors.description: List of users who can edit the runbook (with configure permission). Empty maps to all users.

external_params.description: Runbook parameters defined via JSON path used to extract the parameter's value from an external payload, encoded as JSON.

external_params_full.description: Complete external parameter configuration returned by the API, including server-added fields. Shows diffs when external drift is detected and when configuration changes.

description.description: A description for the external parameter.

export.description: Whether the parameter value is exported to downstream actions.

json_path.description: The JSON path used to extract the parameter value from the external payload.

name.description: The name of the external parameter.

source.description: The source of the external parameter.

value.description: The default value of the external parameter.

external_params_list.description: Runbook parameters defined via JSON path used to extract the parameter's value from an external payload, as a native Terraform list. Provides better plan changes and drift detection than the deprecated `external_params` JSON string. Cannot be used together with `external_params`.

filter_resource_to_action.description: Determines whether parameters containing resources are exported to actions

is_run_output_persisted.description: A boolean value denoting whether or not cell outputs should be persisted when running a runbook

labels.description: A list of strings by which runbooks can be grouped

name.description: The name of the runbook

params.description: Named variables to pass to a runbook, encoded as JSON. Shows diffs only when configuration changes.

params_full.description: Complete parameter configuration returned by the API, including server-added fields. Shows diffs when external drift is detected and when configuration changes.

params_groups.description: Categorized parameter lists. Defaults to null if not specified.

export.description: Whether the parameter value is exported to downstream actions.

name.description: The name of the parameter.

value.description: The default value of the parameter.

params_list.description: Named variables to pass to a runbook as a native Terraform list. Provides better plan changes and drift detection than the deprecated `params` JSON string. Cannot be used together with `params`.

secret_names.description: A list of strings that contains the name of the secrets that are used in the runbook.

timeout_ms.description: Maximum time to wait for runbook execution, in milliseconds

block.description: Runbook resource for interactive notebooks of Op commands and user documentation

integration_name.description: The name/symbol of a platform integration.

name.description: The name/symbol for the object within backend and the op language (must be unique, only alphanumeric/underscore).

vault_secret_key.description: The specific key within the secret data to retrieve.

vault_secret_path.description: The full path in Vault where the secret is stored. It includes the mount path and the subpath. It tells Vault where to look for the secret.

block.description: NVault secret. A secret managed by NVault. Creating it requires an active NVault integration to be configured and enabled.

action_limit.description: The number of simultaneous actions allowed for a permissions group.

administer_permission.description: If a permissions group is allowed to perform "administer" actions.

configure_permission.description: If a permissions group is allowed to perform "configure" actions.

execute_limit.description: The number of simultaneous linux (shell) commands allowed for a permissions group.

identity.description: The email address or provider's (e.g. Okta) group-name for a permissions group.

name.description: The name/symbol for the object within the platform and the op language (must be unique, only alphanumeric/underscore).

view_limit.description: **Deprecated** The number of simultaneous metrics allowed for a permissions group.

block.description: Principal. An authorization group (e.g. Okta groups). Note: Admin privilege (in the platform) is required to create principal objects.

blocks.description: The JSON encoded blocks of the report template

blocks_full.description: Complete blocks configuration returned by the API, including server-added fields. Shows diffs when external drift is detected and when configuration changes.

links.description: The JSON encoded links of a report template with other report templates

links_full.description: Complete links configuration returned by the API, including server-added fields. Shows diffs when external drift is detected and when configuration changes.

name.description: The name of the report template

block.description: Report template resource for creating report templates with blocks and links

description.description: A user-friendly explanation of the resource.

name.description: The name/symbol for the resource within the system (must be unique, only alphanumeric/underscore).

params.description: Named variables to pass to the resource.

value.description: The Op statement that defines a Resource.

block.description: A server or compute resource in the system (e.g. host, pod, container).

allowed_entities.description: The list of users who can run a runbook. Any user can run if left empty.

allowed_resources_query.description: The list of resources on which a runbook can run. No restriction, if left empty.

approvers.description: List of users who can approve runbook execution

category.description: Specifies the category for this runbook. To use categories, make sure your platform administrator has enabled the `ENABLE_RUNBOOK_CATEGORIES` setting. Once enabled, you can organize your runbooks by assigning them a category.

cells_full.description: Complete cells configuration returned by the API, including server-added fields. Shows diffs when external drift is detected and when configuration changes.

md.description: The Markdown content for this cell. Mutually exclusive with `op`.

name.description: The name of the cell.

op.description: The Op command content for this cell. Mutually exclusive with `md`.

cells_list.description: The data cells inside a runbook as a native Terraform list. Each cell is either a Markdown cell (set `md`) or an Op command cell (set `op`). Only one of `op` or `md` must be set per cell. Provides better plan changes and drift detection than the deprecated `cells` JSON string. Cannot be used together with `cells`.

timeout_ms.description: Maximum time to wait for runbook execution, in milliseconds

administrator_grants_create_user.description: If administrator is allowed to grant create user permissions.

administrator_grants_create_user_token.description: If administrator is allowed to grant create user token permissions.

administrator_grants_read_user_token.description: If administrator is allowed to grant read user token permissions.

administrator_grants_regenerate_user_tok.description: If administrator is allowed to grant regenerate user token permissions.

approval_allow_individual_notification.description: If approval individual notifications are allowed.

approval_editable_allowed_resource_query.description: If approval editable allowed resource query is enabled.

approval_feature_enabled.description: If the approval feature is enabled.

approval_optional_request_ticket_url.description: If approval request ticket URL is optional.

environment_name_background.description: The background color for the environment name.

external_audit_storage_batch_period_sec.description: External audit storage batch period in seconds.

external_audit_storage_enabled.description: If external audit storage is enabled.

external_audit_storage_type.description: **Deprecated** The type of external audit storage.

name.description: The name/symbol for the object within backend and the op language (must be unique, only alphanumeric/underscore). For system_settings, this must be 'system_settings'.

parallel_runs_fired_by_time_triggers.description: Number of parallel runs fired by time triggers.

run_approval_expiry_time.description: Run approval expiry time in minutes.

runbook_ad_hoc_approval_request_enabled.description: If runbook ad-hoc approval requests are enabled.

runbook_approval_request_expiry_time.description: Runbook approval request expiry time in minutes.

time_trigger_permissions_user.description: The user for time trigger permissions.

block.description: System-level settings. Note: there must only be one instance of this terraform resource named 'system_settings'.

enabled.description: If the trigger is currently enabled or disabled.

end_date.description: When the trigger condition stops firing. (defaults to unset, e.g. no stop date). The accepted format is ISO8601, e.g. '2029-02-17T08:08:01'.

fire_query.description: The trigger condition for the TimeTrigger (e.g. 'every 5m').

name.description: The name/symbol for the trigger within backend and the op language (must be unique, only alphanumeric/underscore).

start_date.description: When the trigger condition starts firing (defaults to current time when provider starts). The accepted format is ISO8601, e.g. '2024-02-17T08:08:01'.
